relative_path: Protocols, instructions/Tissue dissociation, organoids and iPDCs/Organoids to FFPE blocks.docx
full_text:
Organoids to FFPE blocks

Organoids cultured on a 48-well plate, embedded in omentum gel are fixed and processed to FFPE blocks as follows:

Prepare 50 mL of 4% formaldehyde (5 mL of formaldehyde + 45 mL 1X PBS)

Remove the culture medium, wash the wells with 1x PBS

Add 400 uL of 4% formaldehyde per well, and incubate at room temperature for 1 hr

Remove the formaldehyde into a waste tube, wash the wells 3X with 1x PBS

Detach the matrix from the wells using a spatula and leave the drops floating in their wells until next step. Ideally, 4 wells are pooled into one block.

Melt the Histogel (in +4C, lab fridge) as follows:

Place the histogel tube in a glass container filled with tap water

Microwave at 800W, 3x 30 seconds

Leave the histogel to incubate in the warm water until completely melted.

Add 5 uL of Hematoxylin into the cap of an opened clean 1.5 mL eppendorf. Add 200 uL of melted histogel and gently mix / swirl the content until Hematoxylin is evenly mixed with the histogel. NOTE! Do not spend too much time on this, as the histogel might start solidifying.

Lift the detached matrix drops from the wells into the histogel-Hematoxylin mix using spatula. The orientation of the drop does not matter, just try to avoid the drop getting on the sides.

Leave the eppendorf open, place on a holder and transfer to +4C fridge to solidify for minimum 1 hr (can be left overnight).

Using the thinnest scalpel on the lab bench, gently detach the solidified histogel from the Eppendorf cap and transfer it to a pre-labelled histo cassette. Use the same histocasettes as for the patient-derived tissues.

Transfer the left-over 4 % formalin into a small plastic container (lab cupboard), place the histocassette into the container and fix the histogel plug for 48 hours at +4C room rocker.

After the formalin fixing, transfer the cassettes into (cold) 70% EtoH and store at +4C up to one week or proceed with the processing into paraffin blocks. The program for tumor tissue processing and organoids is the same.
